notable_pick_trades: Acquired 4th-round pick from Saints in Devaughn Vele trade. No 6th-round pick (sent to Jets in John Franklin-Myers trade). Two projected compensatory picks (7th rounders).
roster_context: Elite roster coming off 14-3 season and AFC Championship Game loss to Patriots. Bo Nix is the franchise QB on a cheap rookie deal (Year 3 of 4). Defense led the NFL in sacks for the second straight year. Offense needs more playmakers — WR room had second-most drops in the NFL, TE position underperformed, and RB room was gutted by injury. Finally free of Russell Wilson dead money ($85M over two years). Approximately $25M in cap space pre-cuts with ability to create $30M+ more via restructures. Super Bowl window is NOW.

needs.explanation: J.K. Dobbins (UFA) is likely gone or only re-signed as a complementary piece. Jaleel McLaughlin was not tendered as an RFA and is now an unrestricted free agent. Only RJ Harvey is under contract, and he struggled as a north-south runner in his rookie year (3.7 YPC) despite 12 TDs. Sean Payton explicitly identified RB as a 'must' at the NFL Combine. Denver is targeting premium free agent backs like Kenneth Walker III, Travis Etienne, or Tyler Allgeier, and scouted RB prospects at the Combine. This is a day-one priority.
needs.depth: Tyler Badie (expected ERFA tender at $1.075M), Cody Schrader, Deuce Vaughn. Extremely thin.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 complement to FA signing, or Round 1 if top RB falls

needs.explanation: The tight end position has held back Payton's offense for three straight years. Evan Engram underwhelmed badly (461 yards, 1 TD) and is a cut candidate who could save $6.5M post-June 1. Adam Trautman (UFA) was the most consistent TE but offers little as a receiver. Lucas Krull likely not tendered. Denver needs a TE who can both block and receive — a true Y tight end. Team has met with draft prospects and is targeting free agents like Cade Otton, David Njoku, or Dallas Goedert. Payton named TE as a top need at the Combine.
needs.depth: Nate Adkins (RFA, injury-plagued), Lucas Krull (likely not tendered). Room is barren.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (Kenyon Sadiq, Eli Stowers among prospects linked to DEN)

needs.explanation: Inside linebacker has been a chronic weakness. Alex Singleton (UFA, 32) likely departs. Justin Strnad (UFA) may leave seeking a starting role elsewhere. Dre Greenlaw is a potential cut candidate ($6-8M savings) after underperforming post-Achilles tear (53 tackles in 10 games). Drew Sanders, the 2023 2nd-round pick, has played only 4 games in two years due to Achilles and foot injuries. Payton named LB as a top need at the Combine. The 2026 draft class is deep at LB, and Denver is connected to Anthony Hill Jr. (Texas) at pick 30.
needs.depth: Drew Sanders (major injury concerns), Justin Strnad (UFA). Dangerously thin and unreliable.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (Anthony Hill Jr., Sonny Styles among linked prospects)

needs.explanation: Courtland Sutton (30, 1,000 yards but 8 drops, declining separation) and Troy Franklin (65 catches, 709 yards, 6 TDs but tied for 2nd-most drops in NFL with 8) are the top two. Neither is a true WR1 who dictates matchups. Payton bemoaned drops and wanted more dynamic playmakers. The Broncos tried to acquire Stefon Diggs and Jaylen Waddle last offseason and were unsuccessful. Pat Bryant showed promise but dealt with injuries. Marvin Mims remains limited as a deep threat/gadget player. A WR who can beat man coverage and complement Sutton is needed, potentially via trade (A.J. Brown rumors) or FA (Alec Pierce, Romeo Doubs linked).
needs.depth: Pat Bryant (sophomore, injury-prone), Marvin Mims (gadget/returner), Lil'Jordan Humphrey (UFA). Depth was exposed in AFC Championship.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (KC Concepcion among mock draft links at pick 30)

needs.explanation: John Franklin-Myers (UFA) is expected to sign elsewhere at ~$20M/year, a price Denver won't match after already extending Zach Allen, Nik Bonitto, Jonathon Cooper, D.J. Jones, and Malcolm Roach. JFM had 14.5 sacks in two seasons and was a critical interior pass rusher. Roach and Eyioma Uwazurike can absorb some of JFM's role, but losing a starter of that caliber demands reinforcement. Draft or mid-tier FA addition needed.
needs.current_starter: Zach Allen (Pro Bowler, locked up), D.J. Jones (under contract)
needs.depth: Malcolm Roach (extended, 41 tackles/4 sacks), Eyioma Uwazurike (breakout year), Sai'Vion Jones (Year 2). Adequate but lost top free agent.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 (Lee Hunter, Texas Tech among linked prospects)

needs.explanation: Brandon Jones is entering the final year of his 3-year deal and could be a cap casualty or extension candidate. P.J. Locke (UFA) is likely to depart. Talanoa Hufanga is the starter and was a Pro Bowl alternate, but depth behind him is thin. Devon Key emerged on special teams. Multiple mock drafts project a safety to Denver at pick 30 (Emmanuel McNeil-Warren, Toledo is a popular choice). This is more about long-term planning than an immediate crisis.
needs.current_starter: Talanoa Hufanga (SS), Brandon Jones (FS, contract year)
needs.depth: Devon Key (special teams standout), JL Skinner. Thin behind starters.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3 (McNeil-Warren is consensus mock pick at 30)

needs.explanation: Ben Powers (LG) is a trade candidate with a $17.4M cap hit in a contract year. He missed 9 weeks with injury in 2025 and the line arguably played as well without him. Alex Palczewski filled in admirably and is expected to be tendered as an RFA. Trading Powers saves $8.4M in cap space. Quinn Meinerz (RG) is an All-Pro anchor. Luke Wattenberg (C) was extended. If Powers is dealt, Denver needs IOL depth or a developmental guard.
needs.current_starter: Quinn Meinerz (RG, All-Pro), Ben Powers (LG, trade candidate), Luke Wattenberg (C, extended)
needs.depth: Alex Palczewski (RFA, 10 starts in 2025), Alex Forsyth. Adequate if Palczewski is retained.

needs.explanation: Garett Bolles (LT, Pro Bowler, had best season of career in Year 9) and Mike McGlinchey (RT, under contract through 2027) are both aging but still performing at a high level. Neither is a pressing concern for 2026, but both are on the wrong side of 30. Frank Crum is a developing swing tackle. A developmental tackle prospect makes sense as a day-three investment for the future.
needs.current_starter: Garett Bolles (LT, Pro Bowler), Mike McGlinchey (RT)
needs.depth: Frank Crum (swing tackle, scored playoff TD), Matt Peart (injury). Functional for now.

free_agency_impact: John Franklin-Myers (UFA, IDL) expected to depart at ~$20M/year — increases DL depth need
J.K. Dobbins (UFA, RB) likely gone — RB becomes critical need with only RJ Harvey under contract
Alex Singleton (UFA, LB) likely to depart — deepens LB hole
Justin Strnad (UFA, LB) seeking starting role elsewhere — further thins LB room
P.J. Locke (UFA, S) likely to leave — safety depth becomes a concern
Adam Trautman (UFA, TE) possible departure — TE room even more barren
Jaleel McLaughlin (RFA) NOT tendered — becomes UFA, though Broncos interested in cheaper deal
Ja'Quan McMillian (RFA, CB) tendered at 2nd-round level ($5.8M) — returns as starting nickel, CB not a need
Tyler Badie (ERFA, RB) expected to return on minimum deal — depth piece only
Evan Engram potential cut candidate — could save $6.5M post-June 1 if released
Dre Greenlaw potential cut candidate — could save $6-8M, opens LB spot
Ben Powers potential trade candidate — $8.4M cap savings, Palczewski ready to start
Nik Bonitto, Zach Allen, Jonathon Cooper, Malcolm Roach, Luke Wattenberg all extended in-season — EDGE/DL core locked up
Courtland Sutton extended during training camp — WR1 secured through 2026 with out after
Russell Wilson dead money finally cleared ($85M over 2 years) — full cap flexibility restored
Davis Webb promoted to offensive coordinator/play-caller — scheme continuity but may shift target usage
Trevon Diggs predicted to sign with Broncos per FOX Sports — would add CB depth if realized
scheme_fit_notes: Vance Joseph's 3-4 defense values versatile IDL who can play both 5-tech and 3-tech (JFM's role). LB prospects need to be three-down players — coverage ability is paramount in this scheme, not just stack-and-shed types. EDGE is locked up with Bonitto and Cooper. On offense, Payton's system demands a blocking TE who can also threaten vertically and a complete RB who can pass protect and catch out of the backfield. WR prospects who beat man coverage and create separation are prioritized over pure speed. The OL scheme is power-based with athletic linemen who can pull and get to the second level.
